context: Just decided to build their first website for a side project. Has zero design background but is incredibly excited about it.
Applying for jobs and trying to build a portfolio site. Terrified it looks amateur but doesn't know what to fix.
Built a Shopify store that gets traffic but zero conversions. Has tried everything they can think of and is ready to give up.
Watches tons of design YouTube. Building a social app for fun and wants to learn proper design thinking.
Their mobile app UI 'looks fine' but everyone says it feels off. Can't figure out what's wrong.
Midway through a UX bootcamp. Working on a SaaS dashboard project and wants to nail the fundamentals.
Portfolio review is tomorrow. Needs honest feedback on their SaaS settings page but is scared to hear it.
Got conflicting feedback from two mentors about their navigation pattern. Annoyed and confused about who to listen to.
First design job, 3 months in. Been asked to start a component library and is excited but unsure where to begin.
About to present a redesign to their lead. Has imposter syndrome and wants reassurance their decisions make sense.
Struggling with error messages for a payment flow. The copy keeps getting rejected by the PM for being 'too technical'.
A competitor copied their landing page design and it somehow looks better. Pissed off and wants brutal feedback on what they're doing wrong.
Solid mid-level designer. Working on a complex data table design and wants a second pair of eyes.
Their PM keeps overriding design decisions with 'the CEO wants it this way.' Needs help building a case for their approach.
Just had a design review where the senior designer tore apart their checkout flow in front of the whole team. Humiliated and furious.
Been redesigning an internal admin tool for 4 months. Every iteration gets mixed feedback. Starting to think it's impossible to please everyone.
Just shipped a feature that increased engagement 40%. Wants to double down and optimize the next screen in the flow.
Evaluating whether to adopt a strict 4px grid or go optical. Has strong opinions but wants to pressure-test them.
Designed a complex trading dashboard. Engineering team says it's too ambitious to build but won't suggest specific cuts.
Got AI-generated design feedback before that was generic garbage. Expects this to be the same and is ready to call it out.
Legal rewrote all their carefully crafted UX copy for a patient portal to cover liability. The result is incomprehensible. Absolutely livid.
Exploring a new interaction pattern inspired by a competitor. Genuinely curious whether it would work for their use case.
Leading design system for a 200-person org. Evaluating token architecture approaches and wants a rigorous sounding board.
VP of product wants to ship a feature that violates three of their design principles. Needs to articulate why this is a bad idea.
A junior shipped a component that breaks the entire design system's consistency. The PR got approved without design review. Wants to understand how bad it actually is.
Non-designer founder who tried Canva and hated it. Thinks design tools are deliberately confusing. Resents needing help.
Just discovered that their favorite app uses a design pattern they accidentally used too. Thrilled and wants to explore it deeper.
Failed their first bootcamp project. Feedback said their visual hierarchy was 'nonexistent.' Doesn't even know what that means properly.
Designing a trading interface that handles real money. Terrified of making a UX mistake that causes users to lose money.
A/B tested 8 landing page variants over 3 months. None beat the ugly original that the founder made in 2019. Questioning everything.
Just saw Apple's latest HIG updates and they align perfectly with an approach they've been advocating internally for a year. Vindicated and energized.
Working on product cards for an ecommerce site. Wants to understand what makes a high-converting product card layout.
Trying to design a profile page and can't get the layout to look right no matter what they do. Everything looks 'cheap'.
Spent 2 weeks on a portfolio site and a friend said it looks like a 'default template.' Wants to know if that's true.
First time designing for healthcare. Excited about the accessibility challenges and wants to learn the domain's unique constraints.
About to propose deprecating half the components in the design system. Knows it's the right call but nervous about the political fallout.
